---
title: "Live Music Data Quality Findings"
subtitle: "Market coverage, artist search quality, and cleanup priorities"
author: "Enmanuel De los Santos Cruz"
date: "2026-06-02"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Executive Readout

This project ingests live-music data from Ticketmaster, normalizes messy event, artist,
venue, and market fields, loads the cleaned records into PostgreSQL, and exposes data
health checks through FastAPI and a Streamlit dashboard.

The strongest business finding is that Ticketmaster works well as an event source, but it
does not provide complete venue and artist context on its own. Venue capacity is missing
for every loaded event, and artist keyword search can return tribute shows, themed dance
nights, unrelated keyword matches, or zero results.

::: {.callout-important}
The pipeline is useful because it does more than collect events. It shows which records
are trustworthy, which fields need enrichment, and where source data can mislead a
business user.
:::

## Data Snapshot

| Metric | Result |
| --- | ---: |
| Events loaded | 2,799 |
| Unique artists | 1,220 |
| Unique venues | 215 |
| Average data health score | 84.22 |
| Market pulls | 5 |
| Artist search pulls | 6 |

## What The Pipeline Checks

| Check | Why It Matters |
| --- | --- |
| Missing venue capacity | Capacity is needed for market sizing and demand analysis. |
| Missing artist name | Artist-level reporting breaks when the attraction field is empty. |
| Invalid coordinates | Mapping and market assignment can be wrong without usable coordinates. |
| Possible duplicate artists | Duplicate artist IDs can inflate counts and fragment reporting. |
| Possible duplicate venues | Duplicate venue IDs can hide true venue activity. |
| Market/state issues | Multi-region market labels can create confusing city/state summaries. |

## Market Pull Results

The first analysis pulled five U.S. live-music markets from Ticketmaster: Denver, Austin,
Nashville, New York, and Los Angeles.

| Market | Events | Artists | Venues | Data Health Score | Issues Per Event | Date Range |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | --- |
| Austin, TX | 328 | 279 | 25 | 89.16 | 1.06 | 2026-06-02 to 2027-05-14 |
| Los Angeles, CA | 600 | 405 | 47 | 86.98 | 1.13 | 2026-06-02 to 2026-08-25 |
| Denver, CO | 600 | 409 | 35 | 85.17 | 1.20 | 2026-06-02 to 2026-10-17 |
| Nashville, TN | 600 | 200 | 23 | 81.00 | 1.37 | 2026-06-02 to 2026-08-29 |
| New York, NY | 600 | 223 | 42 | 80.88 | 1.37 | 2026-06-01 to 2026-07-02 |

Austin had the cleanest sample, with the highest average data health score and the
fewest issues per event. Nashville and New York had lower scores because missing artist
names were more common in those pulls.

## Data Quality Findings

| Data Problem | Priority | Records Flagged | Interpretation |
| --- | --- | ---: | --- |
| Venue capacity missing | Warning | 2,799 | Ticketmaster event records do not include capacity, so venues need enrichment from a reference source. |
| Artist name missing | Error | 640 | Some events do not include a clean artist or attraction name. |
| Possible duplicate venue | Warning | 22 | Multiple source venue IDs normalize to the same venue fingerprint. |
| Market/state needs review | Warning | 11 | Some multi-region market labels do not map cleanly to one state. |
| Possible duplicate artist | Warning | 6 | Multiple source artist IDs normalize to the same artist fingerprint. |
| Coordinates need review | Warning | 6 | A small number of venue records have missing or invalid coordinates. |

The biggest enrichment gap is venue capacity. This is not a one-off data-entry problem;
it is a source limitation that appears across every market and artist pull.

## Artist Search Results

The second analysis used Ticketmaster keyword search for six artists: Sabrina Carpenter,
Gracie Abrams, Taylor Swift, girl in red, Kendrick Lamar, and Bad Bunny.

| Search | Rows Returned | Events Found | Artists | Venues | Data Health Score | Main Returned Artist |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | --- |
| Gracie Abrams | 33 | 33 | 1 | 14 | 89.39 | Gracie Abrams |
| Taylor Swift | 31 | 31 | 8 | 28 | 82.58 | Taylorville - A Tribute To Taylor Swift |
| girl in red | 5 | 5 | 4 | 4 | 88.00 | Lily Allen |
| Bad Bunny | 1 | 1 | 0 | 1 | 65.00 | Missing artist metadata |
| Sabrina Carpenter | 1 | 1 | 0 | 1 | 65.00 | Missing artist metadata |
| Kendrick Lamar | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0.00 | No current rows returned |

Gracie Abrams was a clean artist match. Taylor Swift returned mostly tribute and
fan-event listings. girl in red returned ambiguous keyword matches such as Indigo Girls,
Red Not Chili Peppers, Outside Lands, and Lily Allen. Kendrick Lamar returned zero rows,
which the pipeline now records as a completed empty pull instead of treating it as a
failure.

::: {.callout-note}
Artist keyword search is useful for discovery, but it is not enough for reliable
artist analytics. A production pipeline should use attraction IDs or an external artist
reference source for entity matching.
:::

## Business Recommendations

1. Add a venue reference table with verified capacity, venue type, neighborhood, and
   latitude/longitude.
2. Add artist enrichment from MusicBrainz, Spotify, or a curated artist reference file.
3. Track official artist matches separately from tribute shows, fan parties, and themed
   events.
4. Preserve zero-result pulls because they are useful source coverage signals.
5. Continue storing every ingestion run so data health can be tracked over time.

## Portfolio Summary

This project demonstrates a practical data-quality workflow across APIs, databases, and
dashboarding. It can ingest real event data, validate important fields, catch duplicate
candidates, identify enrichment gaps, and translate raw records into business-facing
monitoring.

Resume-ready phrasing:

> Built a live-music data quality pipeline using Python, Pandas, PostgreSQL, FastAPI,
> Streamlit, Docker, and Ticketmaster API data to ingest 2,799 event records, identify
> venue enrichment gaps, flag missing artist metadata, and monitor market and artist
> search quality through SQL views and a dashboard.

## Rendering

Render this Quarto report with:

```bash
quarto render docs/live-music-findings.qmd
```
